body.title: [sprint] CP1-CP6 tags on artifacts as part of the why; audit flags load-bearing misses
body.intention: Artifacts (tickets, proofs, certifications) carry the CP(s) they serve as tags, and an audit flags any load-bearing artifact that carries no CP tag.
body.description:
**Problem:** The values-in-base work (step 1a) placed CP1-CP6 structurally, but placement≠consumption. CP1 consumes as the honesty gate; CP2-CP6 have no consumption path. The existing tags mechanism is the carrier — but CP-tagging is not yet a live convention (the CP1-6 strings in the store today are memory-node IDs in old cognition tickets, not value-tags).

**Proposed shape:** Systematize tagging every artifact with the CP(s) it serves, as part of the *why* (defeasible certification → CP1; revise-on-learning → CP2; friction reduction → CP4). Add an audit check (in /audit-ticket and/or /skills-audit) that flags a load-bearing artifact with no CP tag. Mechanical: presence of ≥1 CP tag (greppable). Judgement: is it the RIGHT CP (model-scored, advisory only this cycle) — be honest which is which.

**Affected files:** `skills/audit-ticket/SKILL.md`, `skills/skills-audit/SKILL.md` (latter may be filed separately) — MEDIUM inertia; possibly `memory_emit.py` tag handling.
**Design rules:** skills-live-in-repo; theory-of-operation (every artifact carries its why); CP3.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the CP-tagging convention + a presence check flagging load-bearing artifacts missing a CP tag. OUT — auto-assigning the correct CP (advisory only); retagging historical Project-Zero artifacts; the /skills-audit build itself.
**Test plan:** load-bearing ticket with no CP tag → flagged; ticket carrying e.g. `CP1` → passes presence check; grep confirms the convention is documented.
**Completion criteria:** the presence check flags a no-CP-tag load-bearing fixture and passes a CP-tagged fixture; the CP-tag convention is documented in the audit skill.
